zh.lifeSummary: 當Willyam W Jane於1548年11月8日出生在英國英格蘭可瓦爾聖 可倫布 梅傑時，他的父親John Jane27歲，母親Lady Anne Ferneley30歲。他於大約1566年與Mary在英國英格蘭可瓦爾聖 辛特結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1613年12月19日在英國多瑟特易維爾 寇特妮享年65歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Willyam W Jane于1548年11月8日出生在英国英格兰可瓦爾聖 可倫布 梅傑时，他的父亲John Jane27岁，母亲Lady Anne Ferneley30岁。他于大约1566年与Mary在英国英格兰可瓦爾聖 辛特结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1613年12月19日在英国多瑟特易維爾 寇特妮享年65岁。

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Carlos, Jaime, Orlando, Alicia, Isidro, Julio, Miguel, Rafael.
English (Devon and Cornwall): variant of Jayne . As a personal name, Jane was not specialized as a female form until the 17th century.

variants.name: Janes
meanings: English (southern): variant of Jane , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s (see Jayne ).

Slovenian and Croatian (of Slovenian origin) (Janeš): variant of Slovenian Janež, from an old equivalent of the Latin personal name Johannes (see John ), or a topographic name or metonymic occupational name from janež ‘anise’. Compare Yanes .
Czech (Janeš, Jáneš): from a pet form of the personal name Jan .

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: English (Gloucestershire) and Welsh (Monmouthshire): from the Middle English male personal name Jan(e), from Old French Jehan, a variant of Johan, which gave rise to the common alternative John . Sometimes the name may also arise from the Welsh variant Ieun of Welsh Ieuan, likewise equivalent to John (compare Evan ).

nameMeanings.name: Willyam
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: When Willyam W Jane was born on 8 November 1548, in St Columb Major, Cornwall, England, United Kingdom, his father, John Jane, was 27 and his mother, Lady Anne Ferneley, was 30. He married Mary about 1566, in St Neot, Cornwall, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died on 19 December 1613, in Iwerne Courtney, Dorset, England, at the age of 65.

ko.lifeSummary: Willyam W Jane(이)가 1548년 11월 8일 영국 잉글랜드 코른월 스트 콜럼 마조르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Jane(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Anne Ferneley(은)는 30세였다. 그는 대략 1566년 영국 잉글랜드 코른월 스트 니오트에서 Mary(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1613년 12월 19일 영국 도르세트 이워니 쿠르트니에서 65세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Willyam W Jane родился 8 ноября 1548 г. в местности ст колумб маджор, корнвалл, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени John Jane было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Anne Ferneley, 30. Он женился на женщине по имени Mary около 1566 г. в местности ст неот, корнвалл, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер 19 декабря 1613 г. в местности ивирн коуртни, дорсет, Англия в возрасте 65 лет.
